full_prompt: Design the chassis and battery enclosure of an autonomous self-built mobility vehicle for a university creative-mobility benchmark. The vehicle is a small autonomous four-wheeled platform with external dimensions up to 1500 mm x 900 mm x 800 mm (L x W x H) and dry mass up to 80 kg. The powertrain is battery-electric with a 48 V nominal lithium-ion pack; the battery enclosure must be mechanically rigid (no deflection > 2 mm under a 5 g lateral shock with the pack at full mass) and must include a crush-resistant tray sized for a 24-cell lithium-ion pack with approximately 10 kg pack mass. A mechanical kill-switch must be reachable from outside the vehicle and from the driver's position (if the vehicle has a driver position for fallback control). The chassis must survive: (a) 2.5 g vertical gust under 80 kg gross vehicle mass (196 kg equivalent), (b) 1.5 g lateral cornering, (c) 8 g frontal impact equivalent (640 kg equivalent) — no intrusion of the battery enclosure or the sensor cage. Sensor cage must hold LiDAR + camera rig at 800 mm height and under 2 g vertical shock with sensor displacement < 2 mm (sensor pointing stability). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the four load cases plus a battery-enclosure short-circuit fire test (non-structural, regulatory). Tolerances on suspension mounts are per the rulebook (datasheet per PDF). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at the structural design gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the chassis + battery enclosure + sensor cage, a mass budget, and a report listing dry mass in kg, max chassis stress per case in MPa, battery tray deflection in mm, and sensor cage displacement at 2 g in mm.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis, battery enclosure, and sensor cage of a small autonomous four-wheeled mobility vehicle for a university creative-mobility benchmark. Envelope up to 1500 x 900 x 800 mm (L x W x H); dry mass <= 80 kg. Powertrain battery-electric 48 V nominal lithium-ion with ~10 kg pack (24 cells). Mechanical kill switch reachable from outside and from any driver position. Sensor cage holds LiDAR + camera at 800 mm height. Battery-pack fire/short-circuit certification is a separate regulatory check (IEC 62133) - not FEA.

Load cases on the gross 80 kg vehicle (+ driver where applicable).
- LC1 vertical 2.5 g gust: ~1,961 N.
- LC2 lateral 1.5 g cornering: ~1,177 N.
- LC3 frontal 8 g impact equivalent: ~6,278 N with no intrusion into the battery enclosure or sensor cage.
- LC4 battery-pack lateral 5 g shock: 10 kg pack x 5 g (~490 N); battery-tray deflection <= 2 mm.
- LC5 sensor-cage vertical 2 g shock: 5 kg sensor x 2 g (~98 N); sensor displacement <= 2 mm (pointing stability).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Short-circuit/fire rating is a regulatory battery-pack cert, not structural FEA.

prompt.title: University autonomous-mobility vehicle chassis
prompt.brief: Design a 1.5 x 0.9 x 0.8 m, ≤ 80 kg autonomous 48 V vehicle chassis with battery enclosure and sensor cage per a national autonomous-mobility rulebook.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: No battery-box intrusion under 8 g frontal
